text:
To find the combined weight of the two pennies, we start by determining the weight of the 2010 penny, given that it is three-quarters of the weight of the 1959 penny.

The weight of the 1959 penny is given as 48 grains.

Since the 2010 penny weighs three-quarters of the 1959 penny, we calculate the weight of the 2010 penny as follows:
\[
\text{Weight of 2010 penny} = \frac{3}{4} \times \text{Weight of 1959 penny}
\]
\[
\text{Weight of 2010 penny} = \frac{3}{4} \times 48 \text{ grains}
\]
\[
\text{Weight of 2010 penny} = 36 \text{ grains}
\]

Now, we add the weights of both pennies to find their combined weight:
\[
\text{Combined weight} = \text{Weight of 1959 penny} + \text{Weight of 2010 penny}
\]
\[
\text{Combined weight} = 48 \text{ grains} + 36 \text{ grains}
\]
\[
\text{Combined weight} = 84 \text{ grains}
\]

Therefore, the combined weight of the two pennies is 84 grains.
